text.title: Frontend Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Frontend Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Variant generation must produce 3 to 5 designs that differ in navigation pattern AND information density — not 5 colour shifts of one design.
rule.rationale: Colour-shift variants give an illusion of choice; real comparison requires structural distinctness.

rule.statement: No variant is selected without a side-by-side screenshot comparison at the target viewport. Decision-by-Figma-only is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Figma decisions miss interaction state and real-content density; screenshots capture both.

rule.statement: Design tokens (colour, spacing, type, radii) are extracted and named before any component is written.
rule.rationale: Components built without tokens hardcode values and become unrefactorable; tokens-first keeps theming and rebrand cheap.

rule.statement: Storybook + framework adapter + addons are installed at pinned versions. No `^` ranges. Reproducible across machines.
rule.rationale: Storybook ecosystem moves fast; un-pinned versions guarantee 'works on my machine' divergence.

rule.statement: Each component ships in its own directory: Component.tsx + Component.stories.tsx + Component.test.tsx. Stories and tests live next to the component, not in parallel trees.
rule.rationale: Parallel trees rot when one moves; colocation makes refactoring atomic.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
